title: System for obtaining patterns on nap fabrics or the like by shearing
abstract: The invention relates to a system for obtaining patterns on nap fabrics or the like by shearing, compromising a template (2) that has the pattern to be made traced on the surface and enables the nap to be removed completely or partially from the material (5) through the passage in a shearing machine, also via one or more passages, at fixed and/or variable heights, in static and/or dynamic form. The template is fixed to the shearing machine or moves with the material. The system in question performs types of mechanical processing which are able to place on the material being processed any type of motif/pattern, without limits of type or dimension, are able to place on the material being processed the motif/pattern, both continuously and discontinuously, without limitations of interrupting and resuming the pattern, both in the length and the width of the material and are able to give the material being processed effects of fixed or variable depth over the entire thickness of the processed material, obtaining a three-dimensional effect, with different levels of depth between one another, which may be continuous or discontinuous, of the motif/pattern.
claims:
Claims 1 ) System for obtaining patterns on nap fabrics or the like by shearing, characterised in that a template is used (2) that has the pattern to be made traced on the surface and enables the nap to be removed completely or partially from the material (5 ) through the passage in a shearing machine, also via one or more passages, at fixed and/or variable heights, in static and/or dynamic form, said template being fixed to the shearing machine or moving with the material, said system being provided for carrying out mechanical processing that is able to place on the material being processed any type of motif/pattern, without limits of type or dimension, is able to place on the material being processed the motif/patter, both continuously and discontinuously, without limitations of interrupting and resuming the pattern, both in the length and the width of the material and is able to give the material being processed effects of fixed or variable depth over the entire thickness of the processed material, obtaining a three-dimensional effect with different levels of depth between one another, of the motif/pattern, also on a single material being processed. 2) System for obtaining patterns on nap fabrics or the like according to claim 1 , characterised in that said template (2) is flexible, is made of various materials like: PET, ferrite, PVC, Plexiglas, cardboard and any other material that meets the required needs, has different thicknesses and dimensions and on the surface thereof there is impressed the ornamental motif/pattern (20) that it is desired to obtain on the material being processed where every single element 

 that makes up the ornamental motif/pattern is found on the surface of the template that is bored in the zones of the pattern that correspond to those zones where it is intended to reduce the length of the nap whereas the template is whole where the nap has to be maintained and the ornamental motif/pattern on the template (2) is made with a full/empty layout. 3 ) System for obtaining patterns on nap fabrics or the like according to claim 1 , characterised in that the material (5) to be processed, whether it be made of natural or synthetic or mixed fabric or of fake or natural fur, is inserted into the shearing machine and the traction mechanism of the shearing machine drags the material, with suitable and calibrated pressure through the passage on a roller system, to the position in which the nap is removed mechanically by the abutment between the wedge (3 ) and shearing machine unit (4), which comprises a cylinder with a rotating blade (40) and a blade support (41 ) . 4) System for obtaining patterns on nap fabrics or the like according to claim 1 , characterised in that, in order to make repetitive, geometric motifs/patterns that extend totally or partially over the width of the material and for a set height or extend vertically for a width defined as square or rectangular layouts that are both single and in groups or lines, said template (2) is positioned so as to remain stationary with the pattern that is located under a shearing cylinder with helical blades (40). 5) System for obtaining patterns on nap fabrics or the like according to claim 4, characterised in that when the template (2) is fixed 

 patterns/motifs that are repetitive in length are obtained on the material and moving the template transversely to the fabric zigzag patterns/motifs are obtained. 6) System for obtaining patterns on nap fabrics or the like according to claims 4 and 5 , characterised in that said pattern system involves the single or combined use of the methods specified in the preceding claims by means of one or more passages and in the absence of a template horizontal streaks of differing length and differing depth are obtained. 7) System for obtaining patterns on nap fabrics or the like according to claim 1 , characterised in that, to obtain more complex designs and dimensions that can occupy all the material or single zones or zones that have a non-repetitive arrangement or a random arrangement on the surface of the material, the material (5) to be processed is introduced into the shearing machine together with the template (2), which is rested on the material, covering the material, and together material and template move and transit coupled and, during the passage between the wedge (3 ) and helical blade cylinder (40), the nap from the material being processed is removed from all the uncovered (empty) parts of the template, making the ornamental motif/pattern on the material . 8) System for obtaining patterns on nap fabrics or the like according to claim 1 , characterised in that it comprises the following processing steps : 

 - preparing the material to be processed and placing the material to be processed on the cloth hooking bar located in the shearing machine supplying unit; - sliding the material as far as the motif/pattern cutting position; - checking the pattern processing request; - fitting the template to be used for the specific motif/pattern; - setting processing parameters through the vertical software installed on the machine; - starting automatic processing (cutting the pattern in the template through removal of nap from the material); - continuous control of supply and recovery; - stopping processing; - discharging the processed material; - removing the template with the motif/pattern from the shearing machine. 9) System for obtaining patterns on nap fabrics or the like according to claim 1 , characterised in that it comprises, when the pattern is complex, the following supplementary steps: after the processing stop step there is a step of recovering the template and refitting the template to make another pattern sector and so on, repeating the operation until either the material has run out or the complete pattern has been obtained, and once finished the material is unloaded and the shearing machine is ready to perform another processing task with a new pattern or the same pattern, but with a new piece of material. 1 0) System for obtaining patterns on nap fabrics or the like according to claim 1 , characterised in that it comprises, when the 

 pattern has different levels of length of nap, the following additional steps : after the processing stop step there is a step in which a second template is inserted to enable the nap to be cut in the zones in which it has to be shorter and so on if there are further levels of nap. 1 ) System for obtaining patterns on nap fabrics or the like according to claim 1 , characterised in that, being mechanical it is totally natural and does not modify the nature of the fabric/material in any of the basic characteristics thereof. 2) System for obtaining patterns on nap fabrics or the like according to claim 1 , characterised in that said system permits synchronous or asynchronous movement between the template- traction of the material-cylinder with helical removal blade (40), a vertical or lateral template movement and/or traction movement of the material with respect to the cylinder (40) and a forward and backward traction movement of the material and/or template. 3 ) System for obtaining patterns on nap fabrics or the like according to claim 1 , characterised in that with the shearing machine a device is coupled for managing positioning and traction of the template that comprises, at each end of the template, a traction bar with which a system of "dandy rolls" is engaged that is able to transmit the movement of the template between wedge (3 ) and shearing machine unit (4) or in that a pair of rotating cylinders or a combination of the "dandy roll'Vrotating cylinder mechanism or characterised by any other mechanical system that i s able to move the template in the passage under the shearing machine unit, said device comprising management and control devices set up to manage 

 via suitable software all the movements of the template by coordinating the fabric dragging system with the shearing machine unit, said software managing processing speed, pattern creation time, the length of the pattern being made and the shearing height. 14) System for obtaining patterns on nap fabrics or the like according to claim 1 , characterised in that in the shearing machine the wedge (3 ) is replaced with a cylinder for better accompanying the template (2) and the material (5 ) under the blades of the cylinder (40) when the patterns are particularly complex for perfect creation of the shape of the pattern.
